entities.name: POLYGON BREEDING
entities.description: A multilocational breeding method characterized by the complete and continued sharing of populations and selections.

entities.name: EAST AFRICA
entities.description: The geographic region where Diversified Bulk Population Breeding originated.

entities.name: DIVERSIFIED BULK POPULATION BREEDING
entities.description: The original name for Polygon Breeding, developed in East Africa for the cultivation of Phaseolus vulgaris.

entities.name: COMMON BEAN
entities.description: Phaseolus vulgaris, the crop used during the initial development of Diversified Bulk Population Breeding.

entities.name: TRIANGLE MODE
entities.description: A specific geometric configuration for implementing the Polygon Breeding method.

entities.name: TETRAGON MODE
entities.description: A specific geometric configuration for implementing the Polygon Breeding method, used in a chickpea programme from 1985-90.

entities.name: PENTAGON MODE
entities.description: A specific geometric configuration for implementing the Polygon Breeding method.

entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: The crop used in a tetragon breeding programme to evaluate selection effectiveness across environments.

entities.name: ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: The external settings where selection effectiveness was measured in the tetragon programme.

entities.name: WIDE-TO-NARROW ADAPTATION
entities.description: The ability of a crop to be adapted to either a broad or a specific range of environments, facilitated by Polygon Breeding.

entities.name: COLLABORATION
entities.description: National and international cooperation enhanced by the use of the Polygon Breeding method.
